message:
test(encryption): add comprehensive unit tests for AES-GCM library (bd-1uiu4)

Coverage improved from 56.2% to 89.0%. Added 10 new test cases:
nonce randomness, empty plaintext, unsupported version, data-too-short,
invalid decrypt key, Error methods, IsKind non-Error, file encrypt/decrypt
round-trip, and bad source paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1uap","title":"Task: Add memory config section","description":"## Purpose
Add configuration options for CM memory integration.

## Config Section
[memory]
enabled = true
include_in_recovery = true    # Include in session recovery context
max_rules = 10                # Cap number of rules to inject
include_anti_patterns = true
include_history = true
query_timeout_seconds = 5     # Timeout for cm command

## Scope
- Add MemoryConfig struct
- Parse from config.toml
- Document in example config

## Acceptance Criteria
- All options configurable
- Sensible defaults
- Integrates with recovery config

## Technical Notes
Follow internal/config patterns","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

-{"id":"bd-1uiu4","title":"Task: Unit tests for encryption library","description":"## Scope
Write comprehensive unit tests for the AES-GCM encryption helper.

## Cases
- Round-trip encrypt/decrypt with known key.
- Wrong key returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Corrupted header / corrupted ciphertext returns a typed/recognizable error.
- Nonce randomness: two encryptions of same plaintext differ.

## Logging
- Tests should log:
  - format version
  - ciphertext length
  - error types for negative cases

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests are hermetic and do not persist secrets.
- Uses only synthetic plaintext fixtures.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:39.966085602Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:48:39.979530616Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-29cp6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1uiu4","depends_on_id":"bd-z7ses","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Coverage improved from 56.2% to 89.0%. All helpers and Error methods now at 100%.","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:33:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1unv","title":"EPIC: Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning","description":"**Agent Effectiveness Scoring and Learning EPIC**

## What It Does
Track agent effectiveness over time and use that data to improve assignments and workflows.

## Architecture (Existing + Planned)
- **Metrics definitions:** `internal/scoring/metrics.go` (core metrics + weights)
- **Score persistence + trends:** `internal/scoring/tracker.go` (JSONL, retention, trend windows)
- **Integration:** `ntm assign` + dashboard panel + robot/analytics export

## Data Sources (Deterministic)
- Task lifecycle signals (beads: assigned → in_progress → closed)
- Retry counts (re-sent prompts / reassignments)
- Time-to-complete (timestamps in task + session logs)
- Token usage (BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator)
- Quality gates (tests/CI pass, regressions)

## Learning / Scoring Rules
- **Cold start:** fallback to neutral baseline until minimum sample threshold met
- **Stability:** use rolling window + decay (recent scores weighted higher)
- **Fairness:** per-task-type normalization to avoid skew from harder tasks
- **No gaming:** cap extreme values, validate sources, log anomalies

## Success Criteria
1. Per-agent metrics tracked and persisted (JSONL)
2. Task completion rates calculated
3. Effectiveness scores computed deterministically
4. Scores influence recommendations with explainable rationale
5. Dashboard shows agent rankings + trends
6. Robot mode exports scoring data
7. Unit tests for scoring algorithm (>=80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify scoring accuracy + recommendation influence

## Notes
- Retention defaults to 90 days; configurable
- Scores must be reproducible for the same input set","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-1uunz","title":"Implement agent launch sequence after exit","description":"Implement agent launch sequence after successful exit.

## Steps
1. Send 'cc' Enter to pane
2. Wait for claude code initialization (6 seconds typical)
3. Verify claude code is ready (check for prompt or ready indicator)

## Timing
- cc command: immediate
- Initialization wait: 6 seconds
- Ready check: poll for up to 10 seconds

## Detection
Look for:
- Claude Code prompt character
- 'Claude Code' banner text
- Ready indicator in output

## Error Handling
- If cc command fails to start: return error with shell output
- If initialization times out: return error with pane content","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:12.846348928Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358201896Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:48.358098031Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uunz","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h0n","title":"Task: Create timeline panel widget for dashboard","description":"## Overview
Implement the visual timeline panel that renders agent activity as horizontal bars.

## Deliverables
1. Create internal/tui/dashboard/panels/timeline.go:
   - TimelinePanel struct implementing Panel interface
   - Horizontal bars per agent showing state over time
   - Color coding: Idle=gray, Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red
   - Time axis at bottom with tick marks

2. Rendering logic:
   - Calculate pixels per time unit based on panel width
   - Render each agent as a horizontal track
   - Fill bars with state colors for each time period
   - Add labels for agent names on left

3. Visual design:
   Agent-1: [████░░░████████░░███████░░░░░░████]
   Agent-2: [░░░░████████░░░░░░░░████████░░░░░░]
   Agent-3: [████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░███████]
            |-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|-----|
            10:00 10:15 10:30 10:45 11:00 11:15

4. Panel controls:
   - Zoom in/out with +/- keys
   - Scroll left/right with arrow keys
   - Jump to now with 'n' key
   - Show event details on hover/select

## Implementation Notes
- Use lipgloss for colored block rendering
- Efficient rendering (only render visible time window)
- Support variable panel width

## Acceptance Criteria
- Timeline renders correctly in dashboard
- Colors match state semantics
- Zoom and scroll work smoothly
- Panel adapts to width changes
- Unit tests for rendering logic","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:27:50.199826149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473303629Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:52:03.473236923Z","close_reason":"Timeline panel implemented: TimelinePanel struct with horizontal bars per agent, color coding (Working=green, Waiting=yellow, Error=red, Idle=gray), time axis with tick marks, zoom (+/-), scroll (arrows), jump to now (n), details (enter). 14 unit tests pass. Visual design matches spec.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3h0n","depends_on_id":"bd-2t7n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3h0n","depends_on_id":"bd-f815","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3h8q","title":"E2E Tests: YAML config driven swarm startup","description":"## Test Coverage
E2E test of starting swarm from YAML config file.

## Test Scenario
1. Create swarm.yaml with full config
2. Run ntm swarm --config=swarm.yaml
3. Verify agents match config
4. Verify prompts match config
5. Verify assignment settings applied

## Test Config (test_swarm.yaml)
```yaml
session: e2e-config-test
agents:
  claude: 2
  codex: 1
options:
  no-user: true
prompts:
  init: "Test initialization prompt"
assignment:
  source: bv
  limit: 3
```

## Verification
- Session name matches config
- Agent count matches config
- Init prompt sent to all agents
- Assignment limit respected

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-3hdd4","title":"Task: Implement synthesis progress indicator component","description":"Implement synthesis progress indicator component for ensemble TUI.

## UI States
- Waiting: show ready/pending counts, start button disabled
- Collecting: progress bar %, per-window status lines with mode code + tier + token counts
- Synthesizing: show strategy, synthesizer mode, input token count, progress bar
- Complete: show success badge and link to results output

## Implementation
- File: internal/tui/synthesis_progress.go
- Types: SynthesisProgress, SynthesisPhase (Waiting/Collecting/Synthesizing/Complete)
- Methods: Init, Update, View
- Include tier chips in per-window lines (CORE/ADV/EXP)

## Dependencies
- Bubble Tea framework
- Collector status (bd-1av7i)
- Mode catalog for code/tier display (bd-3806s)

## Logging Requirements
- Log phase transitions in debug mode
- Log total token counts and percent progress","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T10:32:29.909968701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T03:01:35.148225421Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T03:01:35.147888807Z","close_reason":"Implemented synthesis progress component + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hdd4","depends_on_id":"bd-1av7i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hdd4","depends_on_id":"bd-3806s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3hdd4","depends_on_id":"bd-rylze","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
